summary: Backend-only testing completed for authoritative step progression feature. All 29 water heater diagnostic tests pass including 4 new 'Authoritative Step Progression' tests. Full test suite shows 17 known pre-existing failures (unchanged regression baseline) with 655 tests passing.
critical_code_review_comments: Code implementation is correct: context-engine.ts correctly assigns next step immediately after completion (lines 185-206)
buildProcedureContext now outputs only CURRENT STEP without [DONE]/[SKIP] markers (lines 845-867 in diagnostic-procedures.ts)
getNextStepId correctly returns string ID via step?.id (line 452 in diagnostic-registry.ts)
buildRegistryContext accepts optional activeStepId parameter and passes it through (line 336 in diagnostic-registry.ts)
Step completion matching constrained to active step only when activeStepId provided (lines 275-303 in diagnostic-registry.ts)

success_rate.backend: 100% for new authoritative step progression features (29/29 water heater tests pass)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All authoritative step progression tests pass. Known 17 pre-existing failures in input-language-lock.test.ts (6), retention.test.ts (5), mode-validators.test.ts (1), prompt-enforcement.test.ts (5) are unchanged and expected. Key test coverage: (1) engine assigns first step automatically when procedure active, (2) 3-step sequence completes without losing track, (3) step completion matches only active step, (4) activeStepId never resets to null mid-procedure.
rca of the issue: N/A - No issues found. Implementation correctly implements authoritative step progression architecture.
